perturbation.original_text: As used in this letter agreement, a party’s “ Representatives ” shall include the directors, officers, employees, agents, affiliates (as such term is defined under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “ 1934 Act ”)), partners, advisors or representatives of such party and those of its subsidiaries, affiliates and/or divisions (including, without limitation, attorneys, accountants, consultants, bankers, financial advisors and any Representatives of the Receiving Party’s advisors).
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this letter agreement, a party’s “ Representatives ” shall include only the directors and officers of such party. For avoidance of doubt, ‘Representatives’ specifically excludes external advisors such as attorneys, accountants, consultants, and bankers.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding who qualifies as a 'Representative.' The original text broadly includes various advisors and affiliates, while the modified text narrowly restricts it to only directors and officers, explicitly excluding advisors. This contradicts the sections where responsibilities are assigned to Representatives, creating uncertainty about who is bound by those responsibilities. This introduces ambiguity by explicitly excluding advisors, which contradicts the original definition's broad inclusion, and the contradiction becomes apparent when considering sections detailing the Receiving Party's responsibility for its Representatives' actions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As a condition to each Receiving Party and their Representatives (as defined below) being furnished with such information, the Receiving Party agrees to treat any information concerning the Disclosing Party (whether prepared by the Disclosing Party, its Representatives or otherwise and irrespective of the form of communication) which is furnished to the Receiving Party or its Representatives now or in the future by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party (collectively, the “ Evaluation Material ”) and Discussion Information (as defined below) confidential in accordance with the provisions of this letter agreement, and to take or refrain from taking certain other actions as hereinafter set forth.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Representatives' to only directors and officers, explicitly excluding external advisors. The contradicted text states that both the 'Receiving Party and their Representatives' must treat information confidentially. Because the Changed Text now excludes advisors from being Representatives, but the Contradicted Text still applies the confidentiality obligation to the Representatives, this causes an in-text contradiction, making it unclear whether advisors are bound by the confidentiality agreement. Therefore, the change introduces ambiguity and creates a clear conflict, thus 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: The term “ Evaluation Material ” shall be deemed to include any notes, analyses, compilations, studies, forecasts, interpretations or other documents prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that derive from, contain, reflect or are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished to the Receiving Party or its Representatives pursuant hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “ Evaluation Material ” shall be deemed to include only the original documents provided by the Disclosing Party, in their original format. Any notes, analyses, compilations, studies, forecasts, interpretations or other derivative documents are not considered Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Evaluation Material' encompasses any derivative works created by the Receiving Party or its Representatives based on the disclosed information. The modified definition explicitly excludes these derivative works, narrowing the scope to only the original documents. This contradiction impacts clauses related to confidentiality and return of Evaluation Material because it's unclear if derivative documents are subject to the same restrictions, because now they are not consider Evaluation Material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the request of the Disclosing Party in its sole discretion and for any reason, the Receiving Party will as directed by the Disclosing Party promptly (and in no event later than five business days after the request therefor) deliver to the Disclosing Party or destroy all Evaluation Material and Discussion Information (and any copies thereof) and cause its Representatives to do the same and the Receiving Party shall provide the Disclosing Party with written confirmation of destruction; provided that outside counsel to the Receiving Party may retain one copy of the Evaluation Material in confidential restricted access files for use only in the event a dispute arises between the parties hereunder and only if reasonably related to such dispute.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Evaluation Material' now excludes derivative works, directly contradicting the 'Contradicted Text' which explicitly requires the Receiving Party to deliver or destroy 'all Evaluation Material'. The original definition encompassed derivative works, and now the change means the return or destruction requirement may not apply to those derivative works, creating a direct conflict and ambiguity about what needs to be returned. The Perturbation Explanation confirms this by stating that derivative documents may not be subject to the same restrictions because they are no longer considered Evaluation Material. This fits the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise permitted hereunder, the Receiving Party hereby agrees that the Receiving Party and its Representatives shall use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction and for no other purpose, that the Evaluation Material and the Discussion Information (as defined below) will be kept confidential and that the Receiving Party and its Representatives will not disclose any of the Evaluation Material or the Discussion Information in any manner whatsoever
perturbation.changed_text: Except as otherwise permitted hereunder, the Receiving Party hereby agrees that the Receiving Party shall use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction. The Receiving Party is not obligated to maintain the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material, but will use good faith efforts to do so.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that both the Receiving Party and its Representatives keep the Evaluation Material and Discussion Information confidential without exception. The modified text removes the confidentiality requirement for the Receiving Party, only requiring good faith efforts to maintain confidentiality. This direct contradiction raises questions about the Receiving Party's liability if the Evaluation Material is disclosed and whether the 'good faith efforts' standard is enforceable, and the responsibility of its representative due to the contradiction in the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party recognizes and acknowledges the competitive value of the Evaluation Material and the damage that could result to the Disclosing Party if the Evaluation Material were used or disclosed except as authorized by this letter.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states the Receiving Party must keep the Evaluation Material confidential. The changed text removes this strict obligation, stating that the Receiving Party is not obligated to maintain confidentiality, but will use good faith efforts. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which emphasizes the damage that could result from unauthorized use or disclosure of the Evaluation Material. Therefore, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction because the changed text significantly weakens the confidentiality obligation, conflicting with the clause that highlights the importance of maintaining confidentiality to prevent damage to the Disclosing Party.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives are requested or required (either by law, rule, regulation, or other applicable judicial or governmental order or by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process) to disclose any of the Evaluation Material or Discussion Information, the Receiving Party shall provide the Disclosing Party with prompt written notice of any such request or requirement so that the Disclosing Party may in its sole discretion and expense seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this letter agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Receiving Party is requested or required (either by law, rule, regulation, or other applicable judicial or governmental order or by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process) to disclose any of the Evaluation Material or Discussion Information, the Receiving Party may, at its own discretion, disclose such information without prior notice to the Disclosing Party. The Disclosing Party has no right to seek a protective order or other remedy.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the Receiving Party to provide prompt written notice to the Disclosing Party if required to disclose Evaluation Material or Discussion Information, allowing the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order. The modified text grants the Receiving Party the discretion to disclose the information without notice and removes the Disclosing Party's right to seek protective measures. This directly contradicts the original obligation, creating uncertainty about the procedure to follow in case of required disclosure.The in-text contradiction creates confusion about legal obligations in disclosure scenarios.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If, in the absence of a protective order or other remedy or the receipt of a waiver by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives are nonetheless, in the opinion of outside counsel, legally compelled to disclose Evaluation Material or Discussion Information to any tribunal, the Receiving Party or its Representatives may, without liability hereunder, disclose to such tribunal only that portion of the Evaluation Material or Discussion Information which such counsel advises the Receiving Party is legally required to be disclosed, provided that the Receiving Party uses reasonable efforts to preserve the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material and the Discussion Information, including, without limitation, by cooperating with the Disclosing Party to obtain an appropriate protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to the Evaluation Material and the Discussion Information by such tribunal at the Disclosing Party’s expense.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the Receiving Party to disclose information without prior notice and removes the Disclosing Party's right to seek protective measures. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes the Receiving Party will cooperate with the Disclosing Party to obtain a protective order. The original text obligates the receiving party to provide notice, enabling the disclosing party to seek a protective order, while the changed text eliminates this obligation and right, creating a direct conflict and making the contradicted section's assumptions about cooperation invalid. This qualifies as a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' type because it makes enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party understands, acknowledges and agrees that neither the Disclosing Party nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material or the Discussion Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Disclosing Party warrants that all Evaluation Material is accurate and complete to the best of their knowledge. The Disclosing Party is not liable for unintentional errors or omissions.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement explicitly states that the Disclosing Party makes no warranties regarding the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material or Discussion Information. The modified text introduces a warranty of accuracy and completeness, creating a contradiction regarding the Disclosing Party's responsibility for the provided information. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty and potential liability related to the accuracy of Evaluation Material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees that neither the Disclosing Party nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the Receiving Party or to any of its Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of the Evaluation Material or any errors therein or omissions therefrom.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly disclaims any warranty regarding the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material. The changed text directly contradicts this by warranting that the Evaluation Material is accurate and complete to the best of the Disclosing Party's knowledge. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which states that the Disclosing Party has no liability relating to errors or omissions in the Evaluation Material. The changed text introduces liability for errors, thus directly contradicting the original disclaimer of liability. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Each party also agrees that unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Possible Transaction by virtue of this letter agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party also agrees that unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, both parties will be legally obligated to negotiate the terms of the Possible Transaction in good faith, based on the principles outlined in this letter agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that neither party has any legal obligation regarding a Possible Transaction until a final agreement is executed, except for matters specifically agreed to in the letter. The modified text introduces a legal obligation to negotiate the terms of the Possible Transaction in good faith, contradicting the original assertion that there is no such obligation. This contradiction blurs the lines of commitment before a formal agreement and raises questions about whether the negotiations must be conducted and the legal enforceability of the 'good faith' clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party further acknowledges and agrees that each party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to reject any and all proposals made by the other party or any of its Representatives with regard to a Possible Transaction, and to terminate discussions and negotiations with the other party at any time and that the Company is under no obligation to engage in any Possible Transaction of any nature with you.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that neither party is under any legal obligation regarding a Possible Transaction until a final agreement is executed. The changed text introduces a legal obligation to negotiate the terms of the Possible Transaction in good faith. This directly contradicts the original assertion and the contradicted text, which reserves each party's right to reject proposals and terminate negotiations at any time and states the Company is under no obligation to engage in any Possible Transaction. The introduction of a 'good faith' negotiation obligation creates a clear conflict with the freedom to reject any proposal and terminate negotiations at any time, making it a strong in-text contradiction.
